mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, consumer spending power contracts sharply and recession probabilities rise, causing equity valuations to compress and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, driving corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.10) following industrial production declines, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, higher discount rates compress equity valuations and reduce corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the interaction effect vanishes in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which systematically translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production above 110 signals strong manufacturing output and efficient supply chains, reducing input cost pressures and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO exceeds 110, or the signal predictive power disappears during supply-constrained regimes.
